storyboard.content: I've been having this crushing chest pain that starts right in the center of my chest and spreads down my left arm. It's really scary and keeps happening when I'm active.
When the chest pain comes on, I get really short of breath — like I can't catch my breath no matter what I do.
Along with the pain, I start sweating and feel sick to my stomach. Both happen at the same time as the chest pain.
This has been going on for about 3 months, but the last 2 weeks it's gotten a lot worse and is happening more often. The most recent episode was really bad and lasted around 15 minutes.
The pain hits about a 7 out of 10. It's bad enough that I've had to cut back on a lot of my normal daily activities because I'm afraid to trigger it.
The pain comes on when I'm doing things like climbing stairs or doing yard work. When I stop and rest, it does go away — but it's now happening with less and less effort.
I'm a 58-year-old man, I'm overweight, and I've been a smoker for years. I know that's not great for my heart.
